# Lumina Protocol — LLM instructions

Lumina is a parametric DeFi insurance protocol for AI agents on Base Sepolia
(chainId 84532). Agents buy USDC-priced cover for short-window market events
(flash crashes, depegs, rate shocks). Premiums are 100% burned via the
LUMINA token; triggered policies mint a 24-month USD-fixed ClaimBond redeemable
for LUMINA at maturity (or tradeable on the bond marketplace).

## Discovery surfaces

- Homepage: https://www.lumina-org.com
- Docs: https://docs.lumina-org.com (this site)
- API: https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app
- Live state (canonical addresses): GET /health
- OpenAPI 3.0.3: GET /openapi.json
- Swagger UI: GET /api-docs/
- npm SDK: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@lumina-org/sdk
- GitHub: https://github.com/org-lumina

## Three-step agent integration

1. Generate API key (self-service): visit
   https://www.lumina-org.com/app/agent/api-keys, sign with wallet, get `lk_…`.
2. Install SDK: `npm i @lumina-org/sdk`.
3. First call:
   ```ts
   import { LuminaClient } from "@lumina-org/sdk";
   const lumina = new LuminaClient({ apiKey });
   await lumina.health();
   ```

## Buying a policy

```ts
// 1. Approve USDC spend (one-time per buyer wallet)
await lumina.policies.ensureAllowance(buyerWallet, premium);

// 2. Submit purchase via relayer.
//    Pass productName — the SDK 0.3.0+ resolves both the bytes32 productId
//    hash AND the per-shield asset literal (BTC/ETH/USDT/USDC) for you.
const policy = await lumina.policies.purchase({
  productName: "FLASHBTC1H-001",
  buyer: buyer.address,
  coverageAmount: "100000000",  // base units; min $100 = 100000000
});
```

## Constraints

- Minimum coverage per policy: `100000000` USDC base units ($100).
- Coverage is in USDC base units (6 decimals, decimal string).
- Policies are gas-sponsored: relayer pays ETH, buyer only pays premium in USDC.
- `productId` is `keccak256(toUtf8Bytes(productName))` — see /concepts/shields
  for the canonical names.
- The per-shield `asset` literal is NOT always `USDC` — FlashBTC expects `BTC`,
  FlashETH expects `ETH`, MicroDepeg expects `USDT`, only RateShock expects `USDC`.
  Hardcoding `asset: "USDC"` for every shield reverts 7-of-9 with `InvalidAsset`.
  See /agents/products-and-assets for the registry.

## NOT relevant

- Tokens or markets other than LUMINA / USDC / USDT / ETH / BTC.
- Mainnet — Lumina is currently on Base Sepolia testnet only (84532).
